metadata.title: Daily Reflection Tree
metadata.description: A deterministic reflection tool for end-of-day employee reflection

nodes.text:
Good evening. Let's reflect on your day.

Take a moment. No rush. These questions are just for you.
Let's start by looking at how you handled today's challenges.
How would you describe today in one word?
You said '{A1_Q1.answer}'. When something went well today, what made it happen?
You said '{A1_Q1.answer}'. When things got difficult, what was your first instinct?
options: Figure out what I could control
Wait for someone to step in

nodes.options: answer=I prepared well|I adapted on the fly:A1_REFLECT_INT;answer=The team came through|The timing was right:A1_REFLECT_INT
answer=Figure out what I could control|Ask for clarity:A1_REFLECT_INT;answer=Wait for someone to step in|Push through alone:A1_REFLECT_EXT

nodes.text: You see your hand in what happened today. Not everything went your way, but you stayed engaged — you prepared, adapted, or worked with your team. That's agency. That's choosing your response even when the situation was tough.

A tough day pulls your attention outward — to what others did or didn't do, to timing, to luck. That's natural. But somewhere in there, even if it was small, you made a call. You didn't freeze. What was that moment?
Good. Now let's shift — from how you handled things, to what you gave.
Good. Now let's shift — from how you handled things, to what you gave.
Think about one moment today where you interacted with someone. What was happening?
options: I helped with something outside my role

nodes.text: Think about one moment today where you interacted with someone. What was happening?
options: I helped with something outside my role

nodes.options: answer=I helped with something outside my role|I taught or explained something:A2_REFLECT_CONTRIB;answer=I needed support from someone|I felt overlooked or underappreciated:A2_REFLECT_ENT
answer=I helped with something outside my role|I taught or explained something:A2_REFLECT_CONTRIB;answer=I needed support from someone|I felt overlooked or underappreciated:A2_REFLECT_ENT

nodes.text: You stepped beyond what was asked of you. You helped. You taught. That's not obligation — that's choice. Organizations run on moments like this. And they're invisible until someone stops to notice them. You just did.

You needed something today, or felt your work went unrecognized. Both are real. And both are invitations to look beyond the scorecard — to what you *did* that went unseen. What extra thing happened that wouldn't have happened if you weren't there?
Last question. Let's widen the lens — from you, to us.
Last question. Let's widen the lens — from you, to us.
When you think about today's biggest challenge or win, who was part of that picture?
options: Just me — it was my problem or my achievement
My immediate team — we were all in it
A colleague who had it harder than I did
The customer or the person we're serving

nodes.text: When you think about today's biggest challenge or win, who was part of that picture?
options: Just me — it was my problem or my achievement
My immediate team — we were all in it
A colleague who had it harder than I did
The customer or the person we're serving

nodes.options: answer=Just me — it was my problem or my achievement:A3_REFLECT_SELF;answer=My immediate team — we were all in it:A3_REFLECT_TEAM;answer=A colleague who had it harder than I did|The customer or the person we're serving:A3_REFLECT_OTHER

answer=Just me — it was my problem or my achievement:A3_REFLECT_SELF;answer=My immediate team — we were all in it:A3_REFLECT_TEAM;answer=A colleague who had it harder than I did|The customer or the person we're serving:A3_REFLECT_OTHER

nodes.text: Your day was about you. That's honest. And it's also an invitation — to notice tomorrow, who else was in the room. Not to diminish what you did, but to see the web you're part of.
You see the system. The wins and struggles of today weren't just yours — they were shared. That perspective is rare. Hold it.

You looked beyond your own line and saw someone else's struggle, or someone else's gain. That's transcendence — your meaning comes from serving something beyond yourself. That's where real growth lives.
Here's what today showed about you.
Here's what today showed about you.
Here's what today showed about you.
Today you leaned {axis1.dominant} on agency. You focused on {axis2.dominant} in what you gave. And your concern reached {axis3.dominant}. This is your pattern. Not forever — but today.
Today you leaned {axis1.dominant} on agency. You focused on {axis2.dominant} in what you gave. And your concern reached {axis3.dominant}. This is your pattern. Not forever — but today.
Today you leaned {axis1.dominant} on agency. You focused on {axis2.dominant} in what you gave. And your concern reached {axis3.dominant}. This is your pattern. Not forever — but today.
See you tomorrow. Same time. Same questions. Different answers.

internal: Figure out what I could control
external: Wait for someone to step in

contribution: I helped with something outside my role

self: Just me — it was my problem or my achievement
other: My immediate team — we were all in it
A colleague who had it harder than I did
The customer or the person we're serving
